name: The Rupununi savannah
certaintyNote: The novel names the Rupununi repeatedly as the family's country; this point marks the open savannah plain of the region's south.
note: The grass plain of southern Guyana is the ground the whole book stands on. Across a hundred years the family's story keeps returning to this expanse of savannah, its cattle trails, its scattered settlements and its seasons of flood and burning drought.

sources.ref: Rupununi savannah, Wikipedia, on the grass plain of southern Guyana

name: St Ignatius Mission
certaintyNote: The book's Jesuit mission to the savannah is not named; St Ignatius, the real Rupununi mission founded in 1909 near Lethem, is the place it draws on, so this marks that village.
note: A Jesuit priest arrives to convert the savannah people and presses hard against their own beliefs, a collision that shapes the family for a generation. The real mission behind the scene is St Ignatius, set up by a Catholic priest among the Wapishana and Macushi in the early twentieth century, just south of present-day Lethem.

sources.ref: St. Ignatius, Guyana, Wikipedia, on the Jesuit mission founded in 1909 in the Rupununi

name: The Kanuku Mountains
certaintyNote: The Kanukus are named in the novel as the range rising from the family's savannah; this marks the mountains east of Lethem.
note: The forested range that lifts out of the flat grassland frames the older story, the one of a brother and sister who withdraw to live apart from the settlements around them. The Kanuku Mountains run across the Rupununi and hold some of the richest wildlife in Guyana.

sources.ref: Kanuku Mountains, Wikipedia, on the range in the Upper Takutu-Upper Essequibo region

name: The family ranch
certaintyNote: The family's savannah ranch is not tied to a named property; Dadanawa, the great isolated cattle ranch of the southern Rupununi, stands in as the representative point for that kind of homestead.
note: The founding marriage of a Scottish settler into a savannah family, and the sprawling household of children that follows, belongs to a remote ranch on the open plain. The southern Rupununi's best-known ranch, Dadanawa on the Rupununi River, gives a sense of the scale and isolation of such a place.

sources.ref: Dadanawa Ranch, Wikipedia, on the large isolated cattle ranch of the southern Rupununi

name: Lethem
certaintyNote: Lethem, the border town and regional capital of the Rupununi, is named as the savannah's link to the wider country.
note: The frontier town on the Brazilian border is the hinge between the savannah and everywhere else, the place a traveller passes through leaving the Rupununi behind. Lethem is the administrative centre of southern Guyana, set on the Takutu River across from Brazil.

sources.ref: Lethem, Guyana, Wikipedia, on the border town and regional capital

name: Georgetown
certaintyNote: Georgetown, the coastal capital, is named as the city the modern strand reaches, roughly four hundred kilometres north of the savannah.
note: The present-day thread carries a young man of the family off the savannah and up to the coast, where a job, a love affair and the noise of the capital pull against the quiet of home. Georgetown is Guyana's seat of government on the Atlantic shore, a world away from the Rupununi.

sources.ref: Georgetown, Guyana, Wikipedia, on the capital on the Atlantic coast
